summary: DHH describes realizing that years of sedentary desk work and unhealthy habits like drinking 2+ liters of Coca-Cola daily have taken a toll on his body. He notes that his body has started pushing back, leading him to naturally shift toward healthier habits including drinking water, eating breakfast, exercising with sit-ups and push-ups, and rollerblading to work. He remains self-aware enough to temper his optimism about whether the changes will stick.
key_points: Years of all-day computer work at the office followed by more at home created deeply unhealthy habits
His body essentially forced a correction, making him naturally gravitate toward water over soda
He adopted multiple healthy changes at once: water, breakfast, exercise, active commuting
He closes with characteristic self-deprecation, questioning whether the new habits will last
money_quotes: Working three years with desktop job using the computer all day and then continuing to do so at home all night gives you some rather unhealthy habits.
Drinking 2+ liters of that stuff is definatly not doing wonders for your body, but who cares, right? Well, apparently my body just started.
No reason to get overly optimistic though. Let's see how long it'll last.
key_insight: Sometimes your body forces a course correction that your mind wouldn't have chosen on its own.
